title: YAWM ModMii Edition
requirements: A modded Wii with the Homebrew Channel installed
An SD card or USB drive formatted to FAT32
WAD files you want to install (channels, system files, theme forwarders)
warnings: !!ONLY install WADs made for your Wii's region!! — a US System Menu WAD on a PAL Wii will brick it. Same goes for the wrong region VC/WiiWare titles.
!!Do NOT install System Menu or IOS WADs!! unless you know exactly what you're doing — a bad system WAD can permanently brick the console.
Make a NAND backup before messing with WADs. YAWM can install or uninstall them, but a backup is your only recovery if something goes wrong.
_note: YAWM (Yet Another WAD Manager) ModMii Edition installs, uninstalls, and manages WAD files on the Wii. Use it for safe channels, VC/WiiWare titles, theme forwarders, and cIOS WADs.

steps.title: Before you start
steps.body:
**YAWM ModMii Edition** installs WAD files on your Wii — these are packages for channels, VC/WiiWare titles, theme forwarders, and some system components.

You need WAD files already downloaded somewhere. This guide doesn't download them — it gives you the tool to install whatever WADs you have (from legal backups or safe sources).

**Region matching is critical.** Every WAD has a region (USA, PAL, JPN). Installing a US System Menu WAD on a PAL Wii bricks it instantly. Same goes for VC/WiiWare titles — they either work or they don't, but System Menu and IOS WADs are permanent.

You also need an SD card or USB drive formatted as **FAT32**. YAWM won't see NTFS or exFAT drives.

First, I'll check that your drive is FAT32.

steps.title: Put YAWM on your SD card
steps.body:
Graphite downloads **YAWM ModMii Edition** and unpacks it into the `apps` folder on your SD card.

Press the button and wait for it to finish.

steps.title: Clean up and eject the card
steps.body:
macOS scatters hidden junk files (`.DS_Store`, `._` files) onto SD cards, and Wii homebrew can choke on them.

Press the button, and Graphite strips those files off the card and **safely ejects it for you**. Once it pops off, pull the card out.

steps.title: Move the SD card to your Wii
steps.body:
Take the SD card out of your computer and slot it into the **SD card slot on the front of the Wii**. If you're using a USB drive, plug it into one of the USB ports on the back.

**Make sure your WAD files are on the card** — either on the root or in a `wad` folder. Press **I did this** once the card is in.

steps.title: Launch YAWM ModMii Edition
steps.body:
Power on the Wii and open the **Homebrew Channel**.

Look for **YAWM ModMii Edition** in the app grid and select it. It loads into YAWM's main menu.

If YAWM doesn't appear, make sure the `apps/yawmme` folder exists on your SD card and contains `boot.dol`.

steps.title: Select and install a WAD file
steps.body:
In YAWM's main menu:

1. Select your device (**SD Card** or **USB**) from the list.
2. YAWM scans the device and shows all `.wad` files it finds. Navigate to the one you want to install.
3. Select the WAD file and confirm the installation.
4. YAWM installs it and shows a success message. Press **A** to continue.

Repeat these steps for each WAD file you want to install. If a WAD is already installed, YAWM asks if you want to overwrite it — this is fine for forwarders or updated titles.

To **uninstall** a WAD, select **WAD Manager** from YAWM's main menu, find the installed title, and choose **Uninstall**.

steps.title: You're done
steps.body:
Your WAD files are installed and appear on the Wii Menu (channels) or are available to homebrew (forwarders, cIOS).

**One last warning:** keep your NAND backup somewhere safe. If a bad WAD ever bricks the console, BootMii recovery is the only way back. YAWM can also uninstall most WADs — launch it again, pick **WAD Manager**, and remove the offending title.

If you installed a System Menu theme or forwarder, set up **Priiloader** with brick protection to guard against accidental corruption.
